label: Discord
description: Read and send messages in Discord servers using a bot token from the Discord Developer Portal.

form.title: Connect Discord
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to interact with your Discord server via a bot token.

methods.label: Bot Token
methods.description: Paste a bot token from the Discord Developer Portal. Simplest path — create an app, copy the token, invite the bot to your server, and you're ready.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Discord Bot Token

A bot token gives Anton permission to read and send messages in any server the bot has been invited to.

## 1. Create a Discord Application

1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) and log in.
2. Click **New Application**, give it a name like `Anton`, and click **Create**.

## 2. Create a Bot User

1. In the left sidebar, click **Bot**.
2. Click **Add Bot** → **Yes, do it!**
3. Under the bot's username, click **Reset Token** (you may need to confirm with 2FA).
4. Copy the token — it is shown **only once**. Paste it into the **Bot Token** field below.
5. Under **Privileged Gateway Intents**, enable **Message Content Intent** if you need Anton to read message content.

## 3. Invite the Bot to Your Server

1. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth2 → URL Generator**.
2. Under **Scopes**, check `bot`.
3. Under **Bot Permissions**, check the permissions Anton needs (e.g. **Read Messages/View Channels**, **Send Messages**, **Read Message History**).
4. Copy the generated URL at the bottom and open it in your browser.
5. Select the server you want to add the bot to and click **Authorize**.

## Notes

- The token is equivalent to a password — keep it secret. If it leaks, click **Reset Token** in the Developer Portal to invalidate it immediately.
- The bot can only see channels it has been granted access to via server roles and permissions.
- To access multiple servers, invite the bot to each one using the same URL Generator flow.

fields.name: bot_token
fields.label: Bot Token
fields.description: Found in the Discord Developer Portal → your application → Bot → Reset Token. Shown only once — store it safely.

fields.name: guild_id
fields.label: Server (Guild) ID
fields.description: Optional. The numeric ID of your Discord server. Right-click the server icon in Discord → Copy Server ID (requires Developer Mode enabled in User Settings → Advanced).

fields.name: default_channel_id
fields.label: Default Channel ID
fields.description: Optional. The numeric ID of the channel Anton should use by default. Right-click the channel name → Copy Channel ID.

methods.label: OAuth2 (User Token)
methods.description: Use Discord's OAuth2 flow with your own application credentials to act on behalf of a user. Requires creating an app in the Discord Developer Portal and supplying a client ID and secret.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Discord OAuth2

This method uses Discord's OAuth2 flow to authorize Anton as a user-facing application. It requires you to bring your own OAuth client credentials.

## 1. Create an Application

1. Go to the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) and click **New Application**.
2. Give it a name like `Anton` and click **Create**.

## 2. Configure OAuth2

1. In the left sidebar, click **OAuth2 → General**.
2. Under **Redirects**, add `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect).
3. Copy the **Client ID** from the top of this page.
4. Click **Reset Secret** to generate a **Client Secret** — copy it now (shown once).

## 3. Enter Credentials Below

Paste the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into the fields below, then click **Submit** to launch the browser-based authorization flow.

## Notes

- The `messages.read` scope is limited to DMs for user tokens; for full server access a bot token is recommended instead.
- You can revoke access at any time via [Discord Authorized Apps](https://discord.com/settings/authorized-apps).

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Discord Developer Portal → your application → OAuth2 → General. Listed as Client ID at the top.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: From Discord Developer Portal → your application → OAuth2 → General → Reset Secret. Shown only once.
